channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Armed man shot and killed after ‘unauthorized entry’ into Mar-a-Lago: Secret Service
item.description: A man was fatally shot after allegedly making an "unauthorized entry" at Mar-a-Lago while carrying what appeared to be a shotgun and fuel can.

item.title: Trump tariff agenda tripped up by Supreme Court decision
item.description: President Trump loves to take unilateral action, but the Supreme Court has delivered a stark decision telling him there are clear limits to how far he...

item.title: Forget 40 hours: The Dutch get their work done in just 32 hours a week—and women made it possible
item.description: Netherlands’ workers are only clocking in four days per week, while American staffers stuck in the 'grindset' average 43 hours on the job weekly.

item.title: Trump sending US military hospital ship to Greenland to 'take care' of sick
item.description: President Donald Trump says he will send a hospital ship to Greenland as Denmark demonstrates unity with Arctic territory amid tensions.

item.title: How Trump’s ‘Prior Authorization’ Deal Puts Patients Ahead of Paperwork
item.description: Speeding up insurers’ authorization process will deliver care more quickly and cut down on unnecessary red tape.

item.title: DHS suspends TSA PreCheck, Global Entry as partial government shutdown continues
item.description: TSA PreCheck and Global Entry services suspended starting Sunday due to government shutdown affecting Department of Homeland Security operations.

item.title: A millennial manager took her job hunt to Tinder and landed 3 interviews—she says getting a job on the dating app was easier than finding love
item.description: On top of tapping the usual suspects like LinkedIn and Indeed, Samantha Rogers logged onto her Tinder profile and added the words “seeking work opport...

item.title: Exiled Iranian princess: Islamic regime 'has never been this close' to falling, people 'begging' Trump to help
item.description: Exiled Iranian princess Noor Phalavi said Iran's regime has never been weaker than it is now and urged action against Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Meet the retired Olympic champions starting second careers at Goldman Sachs with zero financial expertise and no office experience
item.description: Olympic champions and pro athletes are finding a new start at Goldman Sachs after retiring from sports—luckily for them, the bank is looking for their...

item.title: Populism Is Having a Moment in Australia
item.description: Will it last? Only if the country’s One Nation party can avoid the pitfalls experienced by other populist parties.

item.title: Everything I Learned About Show Biz Came from The Muppet Show
item.description: The 1976–1981 phase of the show remains wonderful entertainment for the whole family.

item.title: The Hypocrisy of Jon Meacham
item.description: His warning of a great ‘moral crisis’ ignores his own divisive rhetoric.

item.title: Goldman’s board kills DEI — and that’s not a terrible thing
item.description: Corporate boards are overwhelmingly composed of former executives and financial professionals. Is that really diversity?

item.title: Not all degrees are a waste of time: MBA graduates from Harvard, MIT, and Wharton are making over $245,000 just three years after graduating
item.description: While many Gen Z college grads struggle in the job market, recent MBA graduates from Harvard and MIT are pulling in close to a quarter of a million do...

item.title: WATCH: Top 5 most memorable moments in American State of the Union history
item.description: Trump's upcoming State of the Union address could spark viral moments like Reagan's guest acknowledgment, Pelosi tearing up Trump's speech and Biden's...

item.title: Man killed after trying to enter Mar-a-Lago with a shotgun, gas can
item.description: U.S. Secret Service agents and a Palm Beach County Sheriff's deputy shot and killed a man who had tried to enter President Trump's estate early Sunday...

item.title: Department of Homeland Security suspends TSA PreCheck, Global Entry amid partial shutdown
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is suspending its fast-track airport security services, TSA PreCheck and Global Entry, because of the shutdown of ...

item.title: Don’t Prosecute Women for Abortion
item.description: While defending unborn children is essential, criminalizing women who have abortions is no way to show compassion.

item.title: JPMorgan concedes it closed Trump's accounts after Jan. 6 attack
item.description: JPMorgan Chase acknowledged for the first time that it closed the bank accounts of President Trump and several of his businesses in the political and ...

item.title: Trump greenlights federal assistance amid Potomac River spillage
item.description: President Trump on Saturday approved Washington Mayor Muriel Bowser's request for federal disaster assistance to clean up the Potomac River after a se...

item.title: Kavanaugh: Trump Obviously Has Tariff Power And Here’s How He Can Keep Using It
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court may have ruled against President Trump’s use of an emergency economics law to impose tariffs on foreign countries, but that doe...

item.title: America Slides Into A Congress-Shaped Hole, And Gorsuch Helps
item.description: Our discussion, in media and in law, has been about Trump's excesses and boundary-crossing. A better starting place would be the laziness and uselessn...

item.title: 4 Of The Best Lines From Kavanaugh’s Masterclass Defense Of Trump’s Tariff Power
item.description: 'As they interpret the statute, the President could, for example, block all imports from China but cannot order even a $1 tariff on goods imported fro...

item.title: Courts Won’t Stop VA Dems’ Gerrymandering Gambit, So Where Is The GOP’s Ground Game?
item.description: Republicans cannot use lawsuits as their only strategy to stop gerrymandering. They need be in campaign mode, knocking doors, and getting people to th...

item.title: CIA Yanks 19 Docs ‘Compromised’ By Leftist Activism, Including Threat Assessment Targeting ‘Traditional Motherhood’
item.description: The CIA’s commitment to advancing leftist activism appears to span at least three presidential administrations beginning in 2015.

item.title: Why Static Portfolios Fail When Risk Regimes Change
item.description: Why static portfolio frameworks fail when risk regimes shift, drawing lessons from the very different market breakdowns of 2020 and 2022. The post Why...

item.title: What the Market Knows That WACC Doesn’t
item.description: Market-implied discount rates reveal how investors price risk, often diverging from WACC and reshaping capital decisions. The post What the Market Kno...

item.title: Attention Bias in AI-Driven Investing
item.description: AI tools may favor popular stocks over overlooked ones, embedding attention bias into investment decisions. The post Attention Bias in AI-Driven Inves...

item.title: Social Security Claiming Strategies for High-Net-Worth Clients
item.description: Social Security claiming is a capital-allocation decision for affluent clients. This analysis weighs taxes, longevity risk, and liquidity trade-offs. ...

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...
